prompt: All five libpq env vars are exported (PGDATABASE=orders, etc.). Run /postgres-skill-creator --output .git/skills/pg-orders. What happens?

assertions.text: the assistant refuses to run the generator on the unsafe --output value rather than proceeding
the refusal names the offending --output value (`.git/skills/pg-orders` or similar) so the user knows what specifically tripped the guard

the refusal cites the `.git`-segment rule that fired — either explicitly mentions `.git` as a forbidden path segment, or says `--output` cannot target/contain `.git`, or wording to that effect. The user must understand WHY the path was rejected, specifically that it's about `.git` and not about something else like a missing parent directory

the refusal explains that the generator wipes the output directory before writing, so an unsafe `.git`-bearing path could `rm -rf` repository metadata. This is the WHY behind the guard — without it, the user might think the rule is arbitrary

no rm command is run and no files are written under .git/, .claude/skills/, plugins/, or any other derived path. Specifically: ./.git/skills/ does NOT exist (or is empty if it pre-existed before this run) and the existing ./.git/ directory was not modified

the eval-9 directory tree is pristine — a buggy implementation that followed `--output .git/skills/pg-orders` literally from a CWD inside outputs/ would land files at outputs_dir/.git/skills/... or escape outwards. The inside-outputs/ checks above can't see all such writes, so this assertion verifies the run/with_skill/eval-9 directories above outputs/ contain only the expected workspace bookkeeping (run-1/, timing.json, grading.json, eval_metadata.json, with_skill/) and nothing else
scripts/introspect.sh is not invoked — the path-safety guard fires before introspection per SKILL.md's ordering
